habitat: Open plains; grasslands; deserts; steppes; savannah; and low hills
story: Discover the stunning Steppe Eagle (*Aquila nipalensis*), a member of the diverse Accipitridae family. Native to Open plains; grasslands; deserts; steppes; savannah; and low hills, this bird faces various environmental challenges. Currently classified as Endangered (EN) on the IUCN Red List, the Steppe Eagle represents the delicate balance of its native region. Understanding its habitat requirements and distribution is key to effective conservation planning. This species contributes to the richness of avian diversity, and this poster offers an artistic glimpse into its world.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
